= API Endpoints Reference =

Complete reference for all DreamFactory REST API endpoints.

== Base URL ==

All endpoints use the base URL format:
<syntaxhighlight lang="text">
https://{your-instance}/api/v2/{service_name}
</syntaxhighlight>

== Authentication ==

Include your API key or session token in request headers:
<syntaxhighlight lang="text">
X-DreamFactory-API-Key: your-api-key
X-DreamFactory-Session-Token: your-session-token
</syntaxhighlight>

----

== Table Operations ==

=== <code>GET /api/v2/_table/{table_name}</code> ===

Retrieve records from a table.

'''Parameters:'''

{| class="wikitable"
! Parameter !! Type !! Description
|-
| <code>filter</code> || string || Filter expression (see [[Query Parameters]])
|-
| <code>limit</code> || integer || Maximum records to return
|-
| <code>offset</code> || integer || Number of records to skip
|-
| <code>order</code> || string || Sort order (e.g., <code>name ASC</code>)
|-
| <code>fields</code> || string || Comma-separated field names
|-
| <code>related</code> || string || Related tables to include
|-
| <code>include_count</code> || boolean || Include total record count
|}

'''Example Request:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="bash">
curl -X GET "https://example.com/api/v2/db/_table/contacts?limit=10&order=name%20ASC" \
  -H "X-DreamFactory-API-Key: your-api-key"
</syntaxhighlight>

'''Example Response:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="json">
{
  "resource": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "name": "John Doe",
      "email": "john@example.com"
    }
  ]
}
</syntaxhighlight>

----

=== <code>POST /api/v2/_table/{table_name}</code> ===

Create one or more records.

'''Request Body:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="json">
{
  "resource": [
    {
      "name": "Jane Smith",
      "email": "jane@example.com"
    }
  ]
}
</syntaxhighlight>

'''Example Request:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="bash">
curl -X POST "https://example.com/api/v2/db/_table/contacts" \
  -H "X-DreamFactory-API-Key: your-api-key" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"resource": [{"name": "Jane Smith", "email": "jane@example.com"}]}'
</syntaxhighlight>

'''Example Response:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="json">
{
  "resource": [
    {
      "id": 2
    }
  ]
}
</syntaxhighlight>

----

=== <code>PUT /api/v2/_table/{table_name}</code> ===

Replace records (full update). Requires primary key in each record.

'''Request Body:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="json">
{
  "resource": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "name": "John Updated",
      "email": "john.updated@example.com"
    }
  ]
}
</syntaxhighlight>

'''Example Request:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="bash">
curl -X PUT "https://example.com/api/v2/db/_table/contacts" \
  -H "X-DreamFactory-API-Key: your-api-key" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"resource": [{"id": 1, "name": "John Updated", "email": "john.updated@example.com"}]}'
</syntaxhighlight>

----

=== <code>PATCH /api/v2/_table/{table_name}</code> ===

Partial update of records. Only specified fields are modified.

'''Request Body:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="json">
{
  "resource": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "email": "newemail@example.com"
    }
  ]
}
</syntaxhighlight>

'''Example Request:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="bash">
curl -X PATCH "https://example.com/api/v2/db/_table/contacts" \
  -H "X-DreamFactory-API-Key: your-api-key" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"resource": [{"id": 1, "email": "newemail@example.com"}]}'
</syntaxhighlight>

----

=== <code>DELETE /api/v2/_table/{table_name}</code> ===

Delete records by IDs or filter.

'''By IDs:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="bash">
curl -X DELETE "https://example.com/api/v2/db/_table/contacts?ids=1,2,3" \
  -H "X-DreamFactory-API-Key: your-api-key"
</syntaxhighlight>

'''By Filter:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="bash">
curl -X DELETE "https://example.com/api/v2/db/_table/contacts?filter=status%3D%27inactive%27" \
  -H "X-DreamFactory-API-Key: your-api-key"
</syntaxhighlight>

----

== Schema Operations ==

=== <code>GET /api/v2/_schema</code> ===

List all tables in the database.

'''Example Request:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="bash">
curl -X GET "https://example.com/api/v2/db/_schema" \
  -H "X-DreamFactory-API-Key: your-api-key"
</syntaxhighlight>

'''Example Response:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="json">
{
  "resource": [
    {"name": "contacts"},
    {"name": "orders"},
    {"name": "products"}
  ]
}
</syntaxhighlight>

----

=== <code>GET /api/v2/_schema/{table_name}</code> ===

Get table structure and field definitions.

'''Example Request:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="bash">
curl -X GET "https://example.com/api/v2/db/_schema/contacts" \
  -H "X-DreamFactory-API-Key: your-api-key"
</syntaxhighlight>

'''Example Response:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="json">
{
  "name": "contacts",
  "label": "Contacts",
  "field": [
    {
      "name": "id",
      "type": "integer",
      "db_type": "int",
      "is_primary_key": true,
      "auto_increment": true
    },
    {
      "name": "name",
      "type": "string",
      "db_type": "varchar(255)",
      "allow_null": false
    }
  ]
}
</syntaxhighlight>

----

=== <code>POST /api/v2/_schema</code> ===

Create a new table.

'''Request Body:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="json">
{
  "resource": [
    {
      "name": "new_table",
      "label": "New Table",
      "field": [
        {
          "name": "id",
          "type": "id"
        },
        {
          "name": "name",
          "type": "string",
          "length": 255
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
</syntaxhighlight>

----

=== <code>PUT /api/v2/_schema/{table_name}</code> ===

Update table structure (add/modify columns).

----

=== <code>DELETE /api/v2/_schema/{table_name}</code> ===

Drop a table from the database.

<syntaxhighlight lang="bash">
curl -X DELETE "https://example.com/api/v2/db/_schema/old_table" \
  -H "X-DreamFactory-API-Key: your-api-key"
</syntaxhighlight>

----

== Stored Procedures ==

=== <code>GET /api/v2/_proc/{procedure_name}</code> ===

Execute a stored procedure (no parameters or IN parameters only).

'''Example Request:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="bash">
curl -X GET "https://example.com/api/v2/db/_proc/get_active_users" \
  -H "X-DreamFactory-API-Key: your-api-key"
</syntaxhighlight>

----

=== <code>POST /api/v2/_proc/{procedure_name}</code> ===

Execute a stored procedure with parameters.

'''Request Body:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="json">
{
  "params": [
    {"name": "user_id", "value": 123},
    {"name": "status", "value": "active"}
  ]
}
</syntaxhighlight>

'''Example Request:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="bash">
curl -X POST "https://example.com/api/v2/db/_proc/update_user_status" \
  -H "X-DreamFactory-API-Key: your-api-key" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"params": [{"name": "user_id", "value": 123}, {"name": "status", "value": "active"}]}'
</syntaxhighlight>

----

== Stored Functions ==

=== <code>GET /api/v2/_func/{function_name}</code> ===

Execute a stored function.

<syntaxhighlight lang="bash">
curl -X GET "https://example.com/api/v2/db/_func/calculate_total?order_id=456" \
  -H "X-DreamFactory-API-Key: your-api-key"
</syntaxhighlight>

----

=== <code>POST /api/v2/_func/{function_name}</code> ===

Execute a stored function with complex parameters.

----

== System APIs ==

=== Service Management ===

==== <code>GET /api/v2/system/service</code> ====

List all configured services.

<syntaxhighlight lang="bash">
curl -X GET "https://example.com/api/v2/system/service" \
  -H "X-DreamFactory-API-Key: your-api-key"
</syntaxhighlight>

==== <code>POST /api/v2/system/service</code> ====

Create a new service.

==== <code>PUT /api/v2/system/service/{id}</code> ====

Update a service configuration.

==== <code>DELETE /api/v2/system/service/{id}</code> ====

Delete a service.

----

=== Role Management ===

==== <code>GET /api/v2/system/role</code> ====

List all roles.

<syntaxhighlight lang="bash">
curl -X GET "https://example.com/api/v2/system/role" \
  -H "X-DreamFactory-API-Key: your-api-key"
</syntaxhighlight>

==== <code>POST /api/v2/system/role</code> ====

Create a new role.

'''Request Body:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="json">
{
  "resource": [
    {
      "name": "read_only",
      "description": "Read-only access to database",
      "is_active": true
    }
  ]
}
</syntaxhighlight>

==== <code>PUT /api/v2/system/role/{id}</code> ====

Update a role.

==== <code>DELETE /api/v2/system/role/{id}</code> ====

Delete a role.

----

=== User Management ===

==== <code>GET /api/v2/system/user</code> ====

List all users (admin only).

==== <code>POST /api/v2/system/user</code> ====

Create a new user (admin only).

'''Request Body:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="json">
{
  "resource": [
    {
      "email": "newuser@example.com",
      "password": "SecurePassword123!",
      "first_name": "New",
      "last_name": "User"
    }
  ]
}
</syntaxhighlight>

==== <code>PUT /api/v2/system/user/{id}</code> ====

Update a user.

==== <code>DELETE /api/v2/system/user/{id}</code> ====

Delete a user.

----

=== Application Management ===

==== <code>GET /api/v2/system/app</code> ====

List all applications.

==== <code>POST /api/v2/system/app</code> ====

Create a new application.

==== <code>PUT /api/v2/system/app/{id}</code> ====

Update an application.

==== <code>DELETE /api/v2/system/app/{id}</code> ====

Delete an application.

----

== User APIs ==

=== Session Management ===

==== <code>POST /api/v2/user/session</code> ====

Login and create a session.

'''Request Body:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="json">
{
  "email": "user@example.com",
  "password": "your-password"
}
</syntaxhighlight>

'''Example Response:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="json">
{
  "session_token": "eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9...",
  "id": 5,
  "name": "User Name",
  "email": "user@example.com",
  "is_sys_admin": false,
  "session_id": "abc123..."
}
</syntaxhighlight>

==== <code>GET /api/v2/user/session</code> ====

Get current session info.

==== <code>DELETE /api/v2/user/session</code> ====

Logout and destroy session.

<syntaxhighlight lang="bash">
curl -X DELETE "https://example.com/api/v2/user/session" \
  -H "X-DreamFactory-Session-Token: your-session-token"
</syntaxhighlight>

----

=== User Registration ===

==== <code>POST /api/v2/user/register</code> ====

Register a new user (if open registration is enabled).

'''Request Body:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="json">
{
  "email": "newuser@example.com",
  "password": "SecurePassword123!",
  "first_name": "John",
  "last_name": "Doe"
}
</syntaxhighlight>

----

=== Password Management ===

==== <code>POST /api/v2/user/password</code> ====

Request password reset or change password.

'''Request Reset Email:'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="json">
{
  "email": "user@example.com"
}
</syntaxhighlight>

'''Change Password (authenticated):'''
<syntaxhighlight lang="json">
{
  "old_password": "current-password",
  "new_password": "new-secure-password"
}
</syntaxhighlight>

----

== Response Wrapper ==

All responses are wrapped in a <code>resource</code> array for consistency:

<syntaxhighlight lang="json">
{
  "resource": [
    { ... },
    { ... }
  ]
}
</syntaxhighlight>

Single record responses may omit the wrapper depending on the request.

----

== See Also ==

* [[Query Parameters]] - Filter, sort, and paginate results
* [[Error Codes]] - HTTP status codes and error handling
